title: Criminal breach of trust
original_text: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts that property to their own use commits criminal breach of trust.
plain_language: This applies when someone is trusted with property or money and dishonestly uses it for themselves instead of the purpose for which it was entrusted.
example_scenarios: An agent receives money to deposit for a client but keeps it.
A person entrusted with goods for safekeeping sells them without permission.
punishment: Punishment depends on the role of the person entrusted and the facts of the case.
jurisdiction_notes: Police procedure and forum choice depend on the place of incident and amount involved.

title: Cheating
original_text: Whoever deceives any person and fraudulently or dishonestly induces that person to deliver property, or intentionally induces an act or omission causing harm, commits cheating.
plain_language: This covers deception that causes someone to give money, property, consent, or take an action they would not have taken if the truth were known.
example_scenarios: A seller takes payment for a product while never intending to deliver it.
A person uses false identity details to obtain money.
punishment: Punishment varies by the kind of cheating and the resulting harm.
jurisdiction_notes: Cyber-related incidents may also require reporting through cyber crime channels.

act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
title: Application to Magistrate
original_text: An aggrieved person, Protection Officer, or any other person on behalf of the aggrieved person may present an application to the Magistrate seeking relief under this Act.
plain_language: A survivor of domestic violence, or someone acting for them, can ask a Magistrate for protection, residence, monetary, custody, or compensation orders.
example_scenarios: A woman seeks a protection order after threats or physical violence.
A survivor asks for residence and monetary relief.
jurisdiction_notes: Applications are usually made before the Magistrate with local jurisdiction.
source_reference: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005

title: Request for obtaining information
original_text: A person who desires to obtain any information under this Act shall make a request in writing or through electronic means to the public authority.
plain_language: Any person can file an RTI request asking a public authority for information, with enough detail to identify the records requested.
example_scenarios: Requesting copies of municipal road work records.
Asking for status and file notes for a public service application.
jurisdiction_notes: The request should be sent to the correct public information officer.
